data.body: DESIGN NOTE (from repo-snapshot-isolation brainstrom, cross-ref): the rebar MCP server's content-cache janitor uses flock-based locking (gc/lock interlock + optional per-entry reader/writer locks). flock is UNRELIABLE on NFS — fine on local disk / EBS / instance-store, but if the MCP server's snapshot cache (REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR) ever lands on EFS (NFS-backed) in the AWS provisioning, switch the cache locking to link(2)-based locking (or keep the cache on a local/EBS volume). Applies to the snapshot-isolation epic's S2 (cache+janitor). Decide cache volume placement (local/EBS vs EFS) when provisioning the MCP host.
